rows.notes: Top title line is fully legible; no amounts on this line. Handwriting clear and not affected by bleed or shadow.

rows.description: die Novembris 1730 ad eundem diem revolutionum 1731.
rows.notes: Second title line clearly readable; no ambiguity in text; no amounts present.
Section heading clearly visible. Slight uncertainty in the second vowel of first word (looks like 'Arrearagia') but overall unambiguous.
Description legible though older script; amounts columns (10 0 0) are crisp and well aligned. Pounds/shillings/pence values all within valid ranges.

Place name 'Long Wittenham' is clear. Amount appears as '26 12 9'. There is a small trailing flourish after the pence that could be a decorative 'd' — interpreted as no fraction. All numeric columns validate (shillings <20, pence <12).
Description is somewhat uncertain (transcribed as 'Minhinnet'); heavy-script letters ambiguous but context (a place name) suggests this reading. Amount '40 0 0' is clear and unambiguous.
Description 'South Newington rect.' and amount '145 0 0' are very clear. Vertical alignment of numbers is unambiguous and within valid ranges.

Description letters are affected by ink bleed from reverse (per page guidance) making some letters uncertain ('Cuddington' read with moderate confidence). Amount '20 0 0' is clear and aligns with the amount columns.
Description legible though within bleed-affected band (reduced confidence). Amount seen as '14 8 10' and numbers align horizontally; pence (10) is within valid range.

Description partially legible but within the heavy-bleed zone (rows 8–20) so characters uncertain. The diagonal shadow covers the amount columns for rows 10–25: amounts are not readable here and therefore left blank.
Text is faint due to heavy ink bleed (row in 8–20 band). Amount columns fall in the prominent diagonal shadow (rows 10–25) so values are unreadable and intentionally left blank.
Description mostly readable but affected by reverse-side bleed; given row is inside the bleed band (8–20) confidence reduced. Amounts are obscured by diagonal shadow (rows 10–25) so left blank.
Short surname 'Bigg' visible though neighboring ink bleed slightly affects stroke clarity. Amount column is within diagonal shadow (10–25) and not legible; left blank.
Description read as 'Caleb alias Lane' but pen-strokes softened by bleed (row in 8–20). Amounts obscured by diagonal shadow; left blank per page guidance.
Description likely 'Barnister' though letters are affected by bleed — moderate uncertainty. Amounts fall in the shadowed zone (rows 10–25) so blanked.
Reading 'Leaden-Hall' chosen; ink bleed reduces certainty. The diagonal shadow obscures the numeric columns for this and subsequent rows; amounts are therefore left blank.
Short name 'Sadler' visible but within bleed-affected band; amount columns under diagonal shadow (10–25) and unreadable, so blank.
Description 'Hart Hall' readable but faint from bleed; amounts are in the shaded area and not legible, left blank per instructions.
Name reads as 'Napper' though strokes are faint from reverse-side bleed. Amounts are obscured by the diagonal shadow covering rows 10–25 and therefore omitted.
Description appears as 'Clifton Penny' but is within the heavy-bleed region (rows 8–20) making letters uncertain. Amounts fall under the prominent diagonal shadow (10–25); left blank.
Entry 'Brew-House' visible though some fainting occurs. Per page guidance, amounts for rows 10–25 are in the diagonal shadow and unreadable; left blank and confidence set <0.3.
Description uncertain (transcribed as 'Tretchew') because of ink bleed and stylized letters. Amount cells are within the shadowed zone (rows 10–25) and are unreadable; left blank.
Multi-name entry is heavily affected by reverse-side bleed; reading 'Sadtane, Spukes et Meriton' chosen but uncertain. Amounts in diagonal shadow (rows 10–25) are not legible and left blank.
Entry looks like 'Medenham' though some letters faded from bleed. Amount columns are in the shadowed band (rows 10–25); unreadable and intentionally blank.
Final line 'Back Gate, Boyse' visible but amount column area lies in the diagonal shadow (rows 10–25) so numbers cannot be reliably read and are left blank.
